next_upcoming.note: Projected based on 2nd-and-4th-Monday schedule. Agenda not yet published as of collection date.

meeting.schedule_pattern: 2nd and 4th Monday of each month, 6:30 PM. Work sessions on 1st Monday at 3:00 PM.

items.title: Approve Meeting Minutes: March 2, 2026 Work Session and March 9, 2026 Regular Meeting
Uninhabitable structures demolition recommendations at 820 Ridge Road
Rescind demolition ordinance for 4714 Ashton Road
Declaration of 1.53 acres on Westcliff Road as surplus property
Accept property donation on Rogers Drive
Ten-year contract with GFL for operation of Transfer Station
McArthur Road Sports Field Complex construction contract ($13.6M)
Budget amendment for 2025 municipal election cost increase
Commercial airline use agreements and contracts
P26-05 Rezoning at 5210 Arbor Rd from SF-10 to MR-5 (single-family to multi-family residential)
PWC Phase 5 Annexation Area 29 assessment resolution
PWC Phase 5 Annexation Area 30 assessment resolution
PWC Phase 5 Annexation Area 31 assessment resolution
Fayetteville-Cumberland Economic Development Corporation annual report
Public Arts Commission 2025 annual report
Historic Resources Commission 2025 annual report
Office of Community Safety quarterly update
Strategic planning retreat debrief presentation
Senior Technology Literacy Initiative
Firearm safety and responsible gun storage PSA campaign
Water and sewer assessment assistance program expansion

property_tax.trend: Increasing: +$0.04 per year over 3 years (+7.4% annually). Rate rose from $0.4995 (2022) to $0.5795 (2024) per $100 valuation.
property_tax.source: NC LINC / NC Office of State Budget and Management

government_finances_2024.source: NC LINC / NC Office of State Budget and Management

government_finances_2023.source: NC LINC / NC Office of State Budget and Management

key_fiscal_facts: FY2024 total revenues: ~$274.3M; total expenditures: ~$267.0M (surplus of ~$7.3M)
Public safety is largest expenditure at $100.3M (37.6% of total spending)
Property tax levy of $87.9M, with total property valuation of $15.2B
Sales tax revenue of $64.1M is second-largest revenue source
Capital outlay of $55.4M indicates ongoing infrastructure investment
Property tax rate has increased 16% over three years (2022-2024)

population.trend: Slight decline. Population peaked at 212,093 in 2019; declined modestly since. Annual change rate approximately -0.2%.
population.rank_in_state: 6th most populated city in North Carolina
population.source: US Census Bureau Population Estimates Program via census.gov and neilsberg.com

priorities.details: Strengthen workforce training, support small businesses and entrepreneurship, revitalize neighborhoods, expand affordable housing, enhance transportation and infrastructure connectivity, prioritize local contractors, partner with Greater Fayetteville Chamber and Economic Development Department

Support Police Chief Roberto Bryan's community engagement approach, promote Office of Community Safety initiatives addressing root causes, establish neighborhood watch programs, create organizational hub consolidating youth, safety, and anti-crime initiatives. Supported Youth Curfew Ordinance only after criminal penalty language was removed.

Enhance parks, recreational facilities, green spaces, and district beautification through resident engagement via town halls. Promote workforce training to equip residents with skills for future employment.

Promised town hall within first 90 days. Invited constituents to 'call me out' if he fails to deliver. Stated: 'Accountable leadership looks like doing the thing that you promised to do.' Emphasizes 'less talking and more listening.'

Rigorous contractor evaluation based on experience, financial stability, and past performance. Require financial disclosures and client references. Prioritize local contractors with community investment. Prevent contractor abandonment like the $6M project failure.

campaign_platform.municipal_model: Cites Raleigh's 'triple bottom line' approach: environmental stewardship, social equity, and economic strength.
campaign_platform.positive_city_record: Praised council for attracting a titanium plant, establishing a medical school, and passing the lowest property tax rate in 30 years.
campaign_platform.homelessness_position: Advocates shared city-county responsibility through Fayetteville-Cumberland County Continuum of Care partnerships and engaging Cumberland County's 500+ churches.

biography.profession: Self-employed; Speaker; Author; Veteran; Communications professional
biography.military_service: U.S. Army Sergeant, Airborne Expert Field Medic, stationed at Fort Bragg (honorable discharge)
biography.media_career: Nearly a decade as news reporter at WFNC radio; began at WLRD 107.7 FM
education: Bachelor's degrees in Communications, Theater, and Film from Fayetteville State University and Georgia State University
Master of Divinity from Morehouse School of Religion at ITC
Doctor of Ministry in Theology and Philosophy from Morehouse School of Religion at ITC
awards: Order of the Long Leaf Pine from Governor Roy Cooper (August 2023) - highest recognition for service in NC
biography.known_as: The Voice of MLK (professional reenactor of Martin Luther King Jr. speeches)
biography.district_description: Fayetteville's northside and the Ramsey Street corridor

confirmed: Charter member, Fayetteville Ethics Commission (prior to council service)
Planning Commission member (prior to council service)
committee_assignments.current_council_committees: Not publicly listed on city website or Legistar as of collection date. The city website (fayettevillenc.gov) returned 403 errors on council member detail pages.
committee_assignments.note: Ferguson's current council committee assignments could not be confirmed through available online sources. His pre-council board service included the Ethics Commission and Planning Commission.

council_dynamics.observed_divisions: 7-3 split on Mayor Pro Tem vote. Minority bloc (Greene, new members, initially Hondros) focused on transparency and accountability. Lynne Greene predicted 'a lot of six-four votes' over the term.

data_quality.meeting_agenda: high - Full agenda with 21 items retrieved from Legistar for March 23, 2026 meeting. April meeting date projected from schedule pattern but agenda not yet published.
data_quality.fiscal_data: high - Property tax rates (3 years) and detailed FY2024 government finances from NC LINC/OSBM. 30+ financial variables retrieved.
data_quality.campaign_platform: high - Detailed platform from 4 independent sources (CityView NC interview, Ballotpedia, campaign website, Branch.vote). 5 priorities with specific policy positions.
data_quality.committee_assignments: low - Current council committee assignments not publicly available online. Only pre-council board service confirmed (Ethics Commission, Planning Commission). City website blocked automated access.
data_quality.population: medium - 2024 Census estimate available (209,496). Population trend data available but NC LINC population endpoint returned 404.
data_quality.council_dynamics: medium - Mayor Pro Tem vote and general bloc dynamics documented via CityView NC reporting. Specific voting patterns on issues not yet available for new council.
